easy.question: Which of the following is NOT a common component of a live production setup?
In live broadcasting, what does the acronym "CG" stand for?
What is the primary purpose of a vision mixer (or video switcher) in live production?

options.a: Adjusting the volume levels of audio sources

easy.question: Which of the following is NOT a commonly used live streaming platform?
What is the role of a floor manager in live production?

options.c: Directing the talent and crew on set
options.d: Editing the live footage in real-time

easy.question: What is the purpose of a teleprompter in live broadcasting?

options.a: To display live tweets from viewers
options.b: To provide a script for the on-air talent
options.c: To control the audio levels of different sources
options.d: To switch between different camera angles

easy.question: What is the function of a delay unit in live broadcasting?

options.a: To synchronize audio and video signals
options.b: To add special effects to the live footage
options.c: To create a time delay for censorship purposes
options.d: To adjust the color balance of the video feed

easy.question: What does the acronym "IFB" stand for in the context of live production?
What is the purpose of a talkback system in live production?

options.a: To communicate with remote guests via video conferencing
options.b: To provide live commentary during sports events
options.c: To enable communication between the director and crew members
options.d: To stream live video content to online platforms

easy.question: Which of the following is a key consideration when producing live content for interactive media?

options.a: Pre-recording the entire production for better quality
options.b: Engaging with viewers through real-time interactions
options.c: Limiting the use of social media during the broadcast
options.d: Focusing solely on visual elements rather than audio

average.question: Live streaming is a one-way communication process.
A multicamera setup is commonly used in live broadcasting to capture different angles simultaneously.
Latency refers to the delay between the transmission and reception of a live broadcast.
Chroma keying is a technique used to replace a specific color in a video with another image or background.
Adaptive bitrate streaming adjusts the quality of a live stream based on the viewer's internet connection.
A cue sheet is a document that outlines the order of events and timing for a live production.
Closed captions are not required for live broadcasts as they are primarily used for prerecorded content.
A "bug" in live broadcasting refers to a small technical glitch or error during the transmission.
Live production workflows typically involve pre-production, production, and post-production stages.

difficult.question: In live broadcasting, the _________ is responsible for overseeing all technical aspects of the production.
The process of adjusting audio levels in real-time during a live production is known as __________.
__________ is a method of transmitting audio and video signals over the internet in real-time.
A __________ is a device used to convert analog video signals into digital signals for live broadcasting.
__________ is a technique used in live production to switch between different video sources seamlessly.
The _________ is responsible for ensuring smooth transitions between different elements of a live production.
__________ is the process of capturing live video footage from a remote location and transmitting it to the broadcasting studio.
In live production, a _________ is used to synchronize different audio and video sources for seamless playback.
A __________ is a graphical element overlaid on the live video feed to display information or branding.
__________ is a technique used in live production to replay a portion of the live footage instantly.
